California
Fair POlitical
Practices Commission
February 9, 1990
Beth PoIlard
Adrninistrative Ass istant
Town of san Anselmo
525 San Anselmo Avenue
San Anselmo, CA 94960
Re: Your Request for Advice
our FiIe No. A-9 0-0 3 7
Dear Ms. Pollard 3
This is in response to your request for advice regarding ap-
plication of the mass mailing provisions of the Political 
Reform
ect (the rAct" ) .1
OUESTIONS
Does inclusion of the name of each councilmember in the
to$rnts letterhead violate the mass nailing provisions of the Act?
CONCLUSION
Incl-usion of the narne of each councilnember in the town's
letterhead does not violate the mass nailing provisions of the
Act. However, the use of such letterhead must also conply with
the requirements of Regutation 18901 (copy enclosed) '
FACTS
You submitted. your request for advice in the standard 1et-
terhead of the Town of san Ansel'mo. on the l-eft side of the page,
inl =iiti""ery 
lists the names of five counci lrnembers, commencing
with the rnayoi. A11 the names are printed in the same typeface
and type siie. 
The names are followed by each councilmember's
title,- 
The name and address of the Town of San Anselmo are
centered at the top of the page- Four city offices with their
respective telepho-ne nunberi ire tisted on the right side of the
paS;. You have requested our advice to ensure that the town's
ie€terfreaa is in conpliance with the provisions of the Act'
1 Gor".nr"nt Code Section B1OO0-9101,5. AI1 statutory references
are to the covernment code unless otherwise indicated. 
cornmission
iegufation= appear at 2 California Code of Regutations. Section
ia6oo, .t ceS.' A11 references to regulations are to Title 2'
Oivision o oi the california Code of Regulations'
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o sacramento cA 95804-0807 . (916) 322'5660


File No. A-9 0-03 7
Paqe 2
ANALYSf S
The Act defines a mass mailing as tlro hundred or more
substantially sirnilar pieces of nrail. 
(Section 82041.5.) section
89001 provides that no newsletter or other mass mailing shall be
sent at public expense. Read together and taken literally, 
these
two sections could be construed to ban all uraillngs of 200 or more
substantially similar documents if the nailings are done at pubJ,ic
expense .
However, the above provisions do not constitute an absolute
ban on the use of names of pubJ-ic officials 
in agency documents.
when the stationery is used for mailings of less than 200
substantially sirnilar pieces in each calendar month, the mass
mailing prohibitions of the Act do not apply. conversely, mail-
ings of more than 200 substantially sinilar pieces in each
calendar nonth must comply with the requirenents of Section 89001
as implemented through iegulation 1890i (copy enciosed).2
A mass mailing is prohibited rrhen it either:
(2) (A) Features an elected officer af-
filiated with the agency lrhich produces or sends
the nailing, or
(B) Includes the name, office, photograph, or
other reference to an elected officer affiliated
with the agency which produces or sends the
nailing, and is prepared or sent in cooperation,
ccnsultation, coordination, or concert l^rith the
-rlected officer;
(3) (A) Any of the costs of distribution 
i-s
paid for with public noneys; or
(B) costs of design, production, and printing
exceeding $50.00 are paid with public moneys, and
the design, production, or printing is done with
the intent of sending the iten other than as
permitted by this regulation.
Regulation 18901(a) (2) and (3).
' 
The Commission approved the repeal and reenactment of
Regulation 18901 in December -1989. The regulation approved by the
Conrnission will be subnitted to the Office of Administrative Law
in the near future. 
Although the new regulation is not in effect
untiL reviewed by the Office of Adninistrative La!, and fited with
the Secretary of State, the new regulation represents the current
policy of the Commission.


FiIe No. A-9 0-037
Page 3
A mass nailing is not prohibited when the public official's
name appears only in the letterhead or logotype of the stationery,
forns, and envelopes of the agency sending the mailing. 
(Regula-
tion 18901(b)(1).) In any such nailing, the names of a1I elected
officers must appear in the same type size, typeface, type co1or,
and location on the page. The mailing may not include any other
reference to the elected official 
such as the elected officer's
photograph, title, 
or signature except as specifically permitted
in Regulation 18901.
The stationery of the Town of San Anselmo includes the names
of all elected officers in the same type size, typeface, type
color and location in its letterhead. Accordingty, it is in
conpliance with the mass rnailing provisions of the Act.
Should you have any further questions regarding this natter,
do not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-59oL.
Sincerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
ceneral counsel

to$rnts letterhead violate the mass nailing provisions of the Act?

CONCLUSION: Incl-usion of the narne of each councilnember in the town's
letterhead does not violate the mass nailing provisions of the
Act. However, the use of such letterhead must also conply with
the requirements of Regutation 18901 (copy enclosed) '
